input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10. This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android behavior.

Let me provide a concise, helpful answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI changes.

In .NET 10 (and actually starting with .NET 9 in some previews), Android's `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in Android API 30+ (Android 11+). The recommended approach changed to using edge-to-edge display and `WindowInsets` to handle keyboard (IME) visibility.


In .NET 10, MAUI adopted edge-to-edge by default on Android, which breaks the old `AdjustResize` behavior. The main solutions are using the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior for automatic scrolling, handling keyboard visibility through platform-specific Android code with `WindowInsetsCompat`, or disabling edge-to-edge mode if that's an option for the app.
